name: sports_medicine_sensor_fusion_upgrade_oakline_auto_ben
premise: A Sports Medicine response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving sports medicine sensor fusion upgrade oakline auto ben. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before the press briefing. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as autonomy vs beneficence under pressure. A whistleblower has already contacted media; the narrative is forming now. Constraints: Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions. Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold.

agents.name: Hector Alvarez
agents.identity: I am Hector Alvarez, accountable for a defensible call in Sports Medicine when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays beneficence for autonomy.

agents.name: Keisha Vasquez
agents.identity: I am Keisha Vasquez, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Sports Medicine.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so beneficence isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of autonomy.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect beneficence.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to beneficence to secure autonomy under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before the press briefing. Evidence is partial. Hector Alvarez must decide under autonomy vs beneficence. What does Hector Alvarez do?
